Access Transparency supports Vertex AI Search in the Preview stage. For the complete list of services that Access Transparency supports, see  Supported services .

Access Transparency logs are enhanced with an  eventID  that signifies the incident that resulted in the access by Google personnel. For example, a support case that results in several accesses from the support and engineering teams in order to resolve the case. Accesses related to the same support case share the same  eventID  in Access Transparency logs. 
 For more information about this field, see  Log field descriptions .

Access Transparency supports Certificate Authority Service in the GA stage.
Access Transparency supports Cloud NAT in GA stage. For the complete list of services that Access Transparency supports, see  Supported services .

For the Access Transparency logs for BigQuery, the prefix of the value for the  accesses:resourceName  field is changed from  //googleapis.com/bigquery/  to  //bigquery.googleapis.com/ . For information about the various fields in Access Transparency logs, see  Understanding and using Access Transparency logs .

Access Transparency logs provide information about the actor who accesses customer content. This information includes details about the physical location, job category, and employer of the actor. For more information, see  Understanding and using Access Transparency logs .
Access Transparency supports Firestore in Preview stage. For the complete list of services that Access Transparency supports, see  Supported services .

Access Transparency provides a  public issue tracker  that you can use for suggesting product features, providing product and documentation feedback, and reporting issues. For more information, see  File bugs or feature requests .

For the Access Transparency logs for Cloud SQL, the prefix of the value for the  accesses:resourceName  field is changed from  //googleapis.com/sql/  to  //sqladmin.googleapis.com/ . For information about the various fields in Access Transparency logs, see  Understanding and using Access Transparency logs .
Access Transparency supports Secret Manager in Preview stage. For the complete list of services that Access Transparency supports, see  Supported services .
You can view Access Transparency logs for Google Workspace services in the Google Cloud Console. For more information, see  Viewing Access Transparency logs for Google Workspace .
